The training seeks to improve both the depth and quality of reporting on international affairs while encouraging journalism that promotes transparency and informed public debate.

Benefits of the Programme

Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support throughout the programme.

The organisers will cover:

  • Return economy-class airfare
  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Local transportation within Ghana

This ensures participants can focus entirely on learning and networking without worrying about travel expenses.

Additional Opportunities After the Training

One of the most exciting aspects of this programme is that participation does not end after the workshop.

Graduates will have the opportunity to apply for:

  • Reporting grants to produce in-depth investigative stories on Africa–China relations.
  • Funding support for publishing high-quality public-interest journalism.
  • Professional mentorship and continued engagement with experts.

Furthermore, three outstanding participants will be selected for a fully funded opportunity to attend the Africa Investigative Journalism Conference (AIJC), scheduled for 10–12 November 2026 in Kenya, where they will present and discuss their reporting projects.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

  • Be based in one of the eligible Anglophone West African countries.
  • Work in print, radio, television, or digital journalism.
  • Report on one or more of the following beats:
    • Politics
    • Economy
    • Business
    • Trade
    • Development
    • Environment
    • Extractive industries
    • Infrastructure
    • International affairs
  • Be available during September–October 2026.
  • Obtain a formal permission letter from their editor allowing participation.
  • Be capable of producing an in-depth story after completing the workshop.

Why Journalists Should Apply

Today’s journalists increasingly cover stories that extend beyond national borders. International trade agreements, foreign investments, infrastructure financing, and geopolitical developments directly affect local communities and national development.

This workshop provides a unique opportunity to strengthen professional skills while learning from leading experts in journalism and international affairs.

Participants will also build valuable regional networks with fellow journalists, exchange experiences, and gain practical tools for producing investigative stories that inform public debate and promote accountability.

For journalists interested in politics, economics, business reporting, development journalism, or investigative reporting, this programme represents an important opportunity for career development and professional growth.

How to Apply

Interested journalists should complete the official online application form provided by the Media Foundation for West Africa. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications carefully and ensure they meet every eligibility requirement before submitting.

Application Deadline: July 30, 2026.

For more information and to submit your application, visit the official MFWA application page:

Discover more global workshop opportunities on OFY here

The post MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Researchers making groundbreaking contributions to the fight against cancer now have the opportunity to gain one of the world’s most prestigious recognitions in oncology. The Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 has officially opened applications, inviting outstanding cancer researchers from across the globe to compete for an award valued at €200,000. Established by the Fondation Gustave Roussy, […]

The post Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers making groundbreaking contributions to the fight against cancer now have the opportunity to gain one of the world’s most prestigious recognitions in oncology. The Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 has officially opened applications, inviting outstanding cancer researchers from across the globe to compete for an award valued at €200,000.

Established by the Fondation Gustave Roussy, with the support of Laboratoires Pierre Fabre as its founding sponsor, the prize recognizes researchers whose scientific discoveries have significantly transformed cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care. Since its launch in 2025, the award has rapidly become one of the most highly respected international distinctions in oncology research, celebrating scientific excellence and innovations that deliver measurable and lasting benefits for cancer patients worldwide.

Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST

About the Gustave Roussy Prize

Cancer remains one of the world’s leading health challenges, affecting millions of people every year. Continuous scientific research has resulted in remarkable progress in early diagnosis, precision medicine, immunotherapy, targeted therapies, prevention strategies, and patient-centered care. The Gustave Roussy Prize was created to recognize researchers whose discoveries have contributed substantially to these advances.

The award was launched in 2025 to commemorate two important milestones:

  • The 150th anniversary of Professor Gustave Roussy’s birth.
  • The centenary of the Gustave Roussy Institute.

Professor Gustave Roussy is widely recognized as a pioneer in cancer care. In 1921, he established one of the world’s first medical consultations dedicated specifically to patients with tumors, laying the foundation for modern comprehensive oncology care. Today, Gustave Roussy is recognized as Europe’s leading cancer center and continues to drive innovation through cutting-edge research, education, and patient care.

Prize Value

The Gustave Roussy Prize carries a total value of €200,000, making it one of the largest international awards dedicated to cancer research.

The funding is divided into two components:

  1. €100,000 awarded directly to the laureate for unrestricted personal use.
  2. €100,000 allocated to support an innovative oncology research project, enabling continued scientific discovery and future breakthroughs.

Purpose of the Award

The prize celebrates scientific breakthroughs that have created tangible improvements in cancer care and patient outcomes.

Research achievements may relate to:

  • Cancer diagnosis
  • Cancer treatment
  • Cancer prevention
  • Clinical oncology
  • Translational medicine
  • Precision medicine
  • Immunotherapy
  • Biomarker discovery
  • Screening technologies
  • Therapeutic innovation
  • Improved quality of patient care
  • Better understanding of cancer biology

The emphasis is not simply on academic achievement but on research that demonstrates measurable clinical impact and has improved the lives of patients.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are welcomed from researchers worldwide regardless of nationality or institutional affiliation.

Eligible candidates include researchers working at:

  • Universities
  • Cancer research institutes
  • Medical schools
  • Hospitals
  • Public research organizations
  • Private research institutions
  • International research centers

Applicants are expected to demonstrate exceptional scientific excellence and a research record that has significantly advanced oncology.

Selection Process

Applications will be evaluated by an independent international jury composed of leading experts in oncology and biomedical research.

Selection will be based exclusively on:

  • Scientific excellence
  • Originality of discoveries
  • Clinical impact
  • Innovation
  • Long-term contribution to oncology
  • Demonstrated benefits for patients

Importantly, evaluations are conducted without regard to discipline, institution, or nationality, ensuring a fair and merit-based selection process.

Why This Prize Matters

Receiving the Gustave Roussy Prize represents more than financial recognition. It places recipients among the world’s leading oncology researchers and highlights discoveries that have changed the landscape of cancer care.

The prize also encourages continued innovation by providing dedicated funding for future research projects, ensuring that today’s breakthroughs continue generating tomorrow’s solutions.

With cancer affecting millions globally, supporting transformative research remains essential for improving survival rates, enhancing quality of life, and developing more effective treatments. This award reinforces the importance of scientific collaboration and recognizes researchers whose work creates lasting benefits for humanity.

Previous Laureates

The prize has already honored internationally renowned scientists.

Previous recipients include:

  • 2025: Professor Charles Swanton
  • 2026: Professor Luis Diaz Jr.

These researchers were recognized for discoveries that have significantly influenced modern oncology and patient treatment.

Award Ceremony

The selected laureate for the 2027 edition will receive the prize on February 4, 2027, during World Cancer Day, highlighting the global importance of cancer research and international collaboration in combating the disease.

How to Apply

Researchers interested in the Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 should complete the official online application before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to present their most impactful scientific achievements, clearly demonstrate clinical relevance, and provide evidence showing how their discoveries have advanced cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care.

Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST.

Interested applicants can find more information through the official Gustave Roussy website

Discover more global awards opportunities on OFY here

 

The post Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Aspen Institute’s Religion & Society Program is now accepting applications for the Michael Cromartie Forum 2026, an exclusive professional development opportunity designed for emerging journalists who want to deepen their understanding of the role religion plays in shaping politics, culture, public policy, and civic life. Taking place in Middleburg, Virginia, USA, from August 30 […]

The post Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aspen Institute’s Religion & Society Program is now accepting applications for the Michael Cromartie Forum 2026, an exclusive professional development opportunity designed for emerging journalists who want to deepen their understanding of the role religion plays in shaping politics, culture, public policy, and civic life.

Taking place in Middleburg, Virginia, USA, from August 30 to September 1, 2026, the Forum provides an intellectually engaging environment where early-career journalists can interact with experienced reporters, editors, scholars, and thought leaders through meaningful conversations rooted in the Aspen Institute’s long-standing tradition of respectful dialogue and critical thinking.

The Michael Cromartie Forum is more than a journalism conference—it is an immersive learning experience that equips participants with new perspectives, stronger reporting skills, and valuable professional networks that can influence their careers for years to come.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and are expected to close around July 1, 2026. Early applications are strongly encouraged.

About the Michael Cromartie Forum

In today’s rapidly evolving media landscape, journalists are expected to cover increasingly complex issues that intersect with politics, religion, identity, ethics, democracy, and culture. Religion continues to influence elections, policymaking, international affairs, education, social movements, and community life, making religious literacy an essential skill for modern journalism.

Recognizing this need, the Aspen Institute’s Religion & Society Program established the Michael Cromartie Forum to help emerging journalists better understand how faith and religion influence public life while strengthening their ability to report accurately, fairly, and thoughtfully on these issues.

Named in honor of the late Michael Cromartie, a respected advocate for improving religion reporting in mainstream media, the Forum continues his legacy by creating space for journalists to engage with scholars and experienced practitioners in open, nonpartisan dialogue. The program emphasizes curiosity, intellectual humility, and rigorous discussion rather than debate or advocacy.

What Participants Can Expect

During the three-day Forum, participants will engage in a series of carefully curated discussions, presentations, and interactive sessions covering topics at the intersection of religion and public life.

The program brings together:

  • Experienced journalists from leading media organizations.
  • Editors with expertise in religion, politics, and public affairs reporting.
  • Distinguished scholars and researchers.
  • Public intellectuals and subject-matter experts.
  • Fellow emerging journalists from diverse backgrounds.

Rather than focusing on breaking news, the Forum encourages participants to examine the deeper historical, cultural, philosophical, and ethical dimensions of current events. This format allows journalists to gain insights that strengthen their long-term reporting and storytelling abilities.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Michael Cromartie Forum offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including:

  • Exposure to leading scholars and experienced journalists.
  • A deeper understanding of religion’s influence on politics, society, and culture.
  • Opportunities to improve reporting on complex public issues.
  • Networking with fellow journalists from different media organizations.
  • Participation in thoughtful, nonpartisan discussions.
  • Membership in a growing community of journalism professionals committed to intellectual excellence.
  • Professional growth through meaningful dialogue and collaborative learning.

The Forum provides participants with the time and space to think critically about journalism beyond the pressures of daily newsroom deadlines.

Who Is the Forum Designed For?

The program is primarily intended for emerging journalists between the ages of 22 and 30, although the organizers clearly state that there are no strict age requirements.

Applicants may include:

  • Print journalists.
  • Broadcast journalists.
  • Digital media reporters.
  • Multimedia journalists.
  • Editors early in their careers.
  • Independent journalists.
  • Freelance reporters.
  • Journalism graduates beginning their professional careers.

The organizers welcome applicants who demonstrate intellectual curiosity, a commitment to high-quality journalism, and an interest in understanding the role religion plays in contemporary society.

Venue and Dates

The 2026 Michael Cromartie Forum will take place in:

Location: Middleburg, Virginia, USA

Dates: August 30 – September 1, 2026

The setting is intentionally designed to encourage focused learning, reflection, relationship-building, and open dialogue among participants.

Why Religion Reporting Matters

Religion remains one of the most influential forces shaping communities across the world. It affects public policy, elections, education, healthcare, international relations, social justice movements, humanitarian work, and cultural identity.

Despite this influence, religion is often misunderstood or underreported in mainstream journalism.

The Michael Cromartie Forum seeks to bridge this gap by helping journalists:

  1. Improve religious literacy.
  2. Better understand diverse faith traditions.
  3. Report accurately on religion-related issues.
  4. Explore how religious beliefs intersect with politics and public life.
  5. Produce more nuanced, balanced, and informed journalism.

These skills are increasingly valuable in today’s interconnected and multicultural world.

Professional Networking Opportunities

One of the Forum’s greatest strengths is the opportunity to build lasting professional relationships.

Participants become part of a network that includes:

  • National and international journalists.
  • Editors.
  • Academic experts.
  • Public policy specialists.
  • Religion scholars.
  • Media leaders.

These connections often lead to future collaborations, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official application through the Aspen Institute Online Form.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning candidates may receive consideration as applications are submitted. Since spaces are limited, applying early significantly improves the chances of being considered.

Expected Application Deadline: Around July 1, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare a thoughtful application that demonstrates their journalism experience, curiosity about public affairs, and commitment to producing accurate, insightful reporting.

Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY here

The post Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
